name: Veal Cutlets, Milan Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Place veal between sheets of waxed paper or foil. Pound with smooth-surfaced mallet or rolling pin to 1/4-inch thickness.
2. In a shallow dish, beat the eggs. In a second shallow dish, place the bread crumbs.
3. Coat each cutlet first with egg, then roll in bread crumbs. Gently press crumbs so they adhere.
4. Heat oil and butter in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the cutlets, frying about 1 minute per side or until golden brown and crisp.
5. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towels. Serve immediately with lemon wedges.
NOTE: Chicken or turkey cutlets can be substituted. Per serving: 427 calories, 26g protein, 12g carbohydrates, 30g fat, 189mg cholesterol (with butter), 314mg sodium.
